contentBlocks.original:
नवग्रह ध्यान श्लोकम्
आदित्याय च सोमाय मङ्गलाय बुधाय च ।
गुरु शुक्र शनिभ्यश्च राहवे केतवे नमः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
navagraha dhyāna ślōkam
ādityāya cha sōmāya maṅgaḻāya budhāya cha ।
guru śukra śanibhyaścha rāhavē kētavē namaḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to Aditya (Sun), Soma (Moon), Mangala (Mars) and Budha (Mercury); to Guru (Jupiter), Shukra (Venus), Shani (Saturn), Rahu and Ketu.

contentBlocks.original:
रविः
जपाकुसुम सङ्काशं काश्यपेयं महाद्युतिम् ।
तमोऽरिं सर्व पापघ्नं प्रणतोस्मि दिवाकरम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
raviḥ
japākusuma saṅkāśaṃ kāśyapēyaṃ mahādyutim ।
tamō'riṃ sarva pāpaghnaṃ praṇatōsmi divākaram ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ravi — I bow to the Sun, red as the hibiscus flower, son of Kashyapa, of great brilliance, enemy of darkness, destroyer of all sins.

contentBlocks.original:
चन्द्रः
दधिशङ्ख तुषाराभं क्षीरार्णव समुद्भवम् (क्षीरोदार्णव सम्भवम्) ।
नमामि शशिनं सोमं शम्भो-र्मकुट भूषणम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chandraḥ
dadhiśaṅkha tuṣārābhaṃ kṣīrārṇava samudbhavam (kṣīrōdārṇava sambhavam) ।
namāmi śaśinaṃ sōmaṃ śambhō-rmakuṭa bhūṣaṇam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Chandra — I bow to the Moon, white as curds, conch and snow, born of the ocean of milk, who adorns the crown of Shambhu (Shiva).

contentBlocks.original:
कुजः
धरणी गर्भ सम्भूतं विद्युत्कान्ति समप्रभम् ।
कुमारं शक्तिहस्तं तं कुजं [मङ्गलं] प्रणमाम्यहम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kujaḥ
dharaṇī garbha sambhūtaṃ vidyutkānti samaprabham ।
kumāraṃ śaktihastaṃ taṃ kujaṃ [maṅgaḻaṃ] praṇamāmyaham ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kuja — I bow to Mars, born of the womb of Earth, bright as lightning, the youthful warrior holding a spear in hand.

contentBlocks.original:
बुधः
प्रियङ्गु कलिकाश्यामं रूपेणा प्रतिमं बुधम् ।
सौम्यं सौम्य (सत्व) गुणोपेतं तं बुधं प्रणमाम्यहम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
budhaḥ
priyaṅgu kalikāśyāmaṃ rūpēṇā pratimaṃ budham ।
saumyaṃ saumya (satva) guṇōpētaṃ taṃ budhaṃ praṇamāmyaham ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Budha — I bow to Mercury, dark as the priyangu blossom, of matchless beauty, endowed with a gentle and virtuous nature.

contentBlocks.original:
गुरुः
देवानां च ऋषीणां च गुरुं काञ्चनसन्निभम् ।
बुद्धिमन्तं त्रिलोकेशं तं नमामि बृहस्पतिम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
guruḥ
dēvānāṃ cha ṛṣīṇāṃ cha guruṃ kāñchanasannibham ।
buddhimantaṃ trilōkēśaṃ taṃ namāmi bṛhaspatim ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Guru — I bow to Jupiter, golden in hue, preceptor of gods and sages, lord of the three worlds, the all-wise Brihaspati.

contentBlocks.original:
शुक्रः
हिमकुन्द मृणालाभं दैत्यानं परमं गुरुम् ।
सर्वशास्त्र प्रवक्तारं भार्गवं प्रणमाम्यहम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śukraḥ
himakunda mṛṇāḻābhaṃ daityānaṃ paramaṃ gurum ।
sarvaśāstra pravaktāraṃ bhārgavaṃ praṇamāmyaham ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Shukra — I bow to Venus (Bhargava), white as snow, jasmine and the lotus stalk, the supreme preceptor of the asuras, knower of all scriptures.

contentBlocks.original:
शनिः
नीलाञ्जन समाभासं रविपुत्रं यमाग्रजम् ।
छाया मार्ताण्ड सम्भूतं तं नमामि शनैश्चरम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śaniḥ
nīlāñjana samābhāsaṃ raviputraṃ yamāgrajam ।
Chāyā mārtāṇḍa sambhūtaṃ taṃ namāmi śanaiścharam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Shani — I bow to Saturn, dark as blue collyrium, son of the Sun, elder brother of Yama, born of Chaya and the Sun.

contentBlocks.original:
राहुः
अर्धकायं महावीरं चन्द्रादित्य विमर्धनम् ।
सिंहिका गर्भ सम्भूतं तं राहुं प्रणमाम्यहम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāhuḥ
ardhakāyaṃ mahāvīraṃ chandrāditya vimardhanam ।
siṃhikā garbha sambhūtaṃ taṃ rāhuṃ praṇamāmyaham ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Rahu — I bow to Rahu, the half-bodied great warrior, who afflicts the Sun and Moon, born of the womb of Simhika.

contentBlocks.original:
केतुः
पलाश पुष्प सङ्काशं तारकाग्रहमस्तकम् ।
रौद्रं रौद्रात्मकं घोरं तं केतुं प्रणमाम्यहम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kētuḥ
palāśa puṣpa saṅkāśaṃ tārakāgrahamastakam ।
raudraṃ raudrātmakaṃ ghōraṃ taṃ kētuṃ praṇamāmyaham ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ketu — I bow to Ketu, bright as the palasha flower, with planets at his head, fierce and terrible in his aspect.

contentBlocks.original:
फलश्रुतिः
इति व्यास मुखोद्गीतं यः पठेत्सु समाहितः ।
दिवा वा यदि वा रात्रौ विघ्नशान्ति-र्भविष्यति ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
phalaśrutiḥ
iti vyāsa mukhōdgītaṃ yaḥ paṭhētsu samāhitaḥ ।
divā vā yadi vā rātrau vighnaśānti-rbhaviṣyati ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Phalashruti — Whoever recites this hymn composed by Vyasa with a focused mind, by day or night, will have all obstacles removed.

contentBlocks.original:
नरनारी-नृपाणां च भवे-द्दुःस्वप्न-नाशनम् ।
ऐश्वर्यमतुलं तेषामारोग्यं पुष्टि वर्धनम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
naranārī-nṛpāṇāṃ cha bhavē-dduḥsvapna-nāśanam ।
aiśvaryamatulaṃ tēṣāmārōgyaṃ puṣṭi vardhanam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: For men, women and kings it destroys evil dreams; it bestows unparalleled prosperity, health and nourishment.

contentBlocks.original:
ग्रहनक्षत्रजाः पीडास्तस्कराग्नि समुद्भवाः ।
तास्सर्वाः प्रशमं यान्ति व्यासो ब्रूते न संशयः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
grahanakṣatrajāḥ pīḍāstaskarāgni samudbhavāḥ ।
tāssarvāḥ praśamaṃ yānti vyāsō brūtē na saṃśayaḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Afflictions born of planets, stars, thieves and fire — all these are pacified. Vyasa declares this without any doubt.

contentBlocks.original: इति व्यास विरचितं नवग्रह स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ।
contentBlocks.transliteration: iti vyāsa virachitaṃ navagraha stōtraṃ sampūrṇam ।
contentBlocks.translation: Thus is the Nava Graha Stotram composed by the sage Vyasa, complete.
